mentions.note: Cramer says don't own it; not good at what they do; better alternative: J&J (pharma/healthcare) down from highs with great balance sheet.
Will lose share to Eli Lilly on pill format. Don't want to be there; sell position.
GLP-1/obesity drug maker but focused on price-cutting strategy; prefer competitor Eli Lilly which excels at drug innovation and development.
Stock crashed on guidance but CEO guidance likely conservative; pill launch phenomenal (170k Americans in 4 weeks), higher-dose injectable trial shows efficacy parity with Eli Lilly.

Interview reveals pill phenomenal uptake (170k Americans in 4 weeks, 50k prescriptions/week); CEO guidance on pricing conservative but strategic for volume growth; higher-dose injectable trial shows efficacy parity.
Caller asked about NVO (GLP-1 player). Cramer warmed to it but prefers Eli Lilly. Novo is hold, not buy, given Lilly's dominance in GLP-1 market with pill form coming.
Caller Gil (VA) owns big position; has oral GLP-1 pill first-to-market advantage vs Eli Lilly spring launch; Cramer met CEO last week, likes them a lot; making a turn with pill head start.
Wegovy pill at $25 copay or $149–$249 removes injection barriers and expands obesity market; 16.6% weight loss matches injectable efficacy; democratizes treatment access.
